title: Verify Linear Transformation
description: Algorithm to check if a given transformation T: ℝ²→ℝ² is linear
code:
FUNCTION IsLinear(T, test_vectors):
 // Test additivity: T(u+v) = T(u) + T(v)
 FOR each pair (u, v) in test_vectors:
 IF T(u + v) ≠ T(u) + T(v):
 RETURN False
 
 // Test homogeneity: T(cv) = cT(v)
 FOR each v in test_vectors:
 FOR each scalar c in [0.5, 1, 2, -1]:
 IF T(c * v) ≠ c * T(v):
 RETURN False
 
 RETURN True

// Example: Test T(x,y) = (2x, 3y)
result = IsLinear(lambda(x,y): (2*x, 3*y), [(1,0), (0,1), (1,1)])
PRINT result // Returns True
explanation: Tests both additivity and homogeneity properties on sample vectors. If all tests pass, transformation is linear. This is practical verification though not a mathematical proof.

complexity.time: O(k×n) where k test vectors, n operations per test

title: Compute Kernel via Gaussian Elimination
description: Find the kernel (null space) of a linear transformation represented by matrix A
explanation: Uses row reduction to find free variables and expresses kernel as span of basis vectors. The number of basis vectors equals nullity (n - rank).

title: Compose Linear Transformations
description: Compute the matrix product for composition of linear transformations
code:
FUNCTION ComposeTransformations(A, B):
 // A is matrix of T: ℝⁿ→ℝᵐ
 // B is matrix of S: ℝᵖ→ℝⁿ
 // Returns matrix of T∘S: ℝᵖ→ℝᵐ
 
 IF A.columns ≠ B.rows:
 RETURN ERROR "Incompatible dimensions"
 
 // Matrix multiplication: result[i][j] = sum(A[i][k] * B[k][j])
 m = A.rows
 n = A.columns // = B.rows
 p = B.columns
 
 C = ZeroMatrix(m, p)
 
 FOR i = 0 TO m-1:
 FOR j = 0 TO p-1:
 sum = 0
 FOR k = 0 TO n-1:
 sum = sum + A[i][k] * B[k][j]
 C[i][j] = sum
 
 RETURN C

// Example: T = [[2,0],[0,2]], S = [[1,1],[0,1]]
// T∘S = [[2,2],[0,2]]
result = ComposeTransformations([[2,0],[0,2]], [[1,1],[0,1]])
explanation: Matrix multiplication corresponds to function composition. The product BA (apply S first, then T) gives the combined transformation.

complexity.time: O(mnp) for m×n and n×p matrices

title: Check Invertibility and Find Inverse
description: Determine if a square linear transformation is invertible and compute its inverse
code:
FUNCTION IsInvertibleAndInverse(A):
 n = A.rows
 
 // Compute determinant
 det = ComputeDeterminant(A)
 
 IF det == 0:
 RETURN (False, None) // Not invertible
 
 // Find inverse using adjugate: A⁻¹ = (1/det) × adj(A)
 adj = Adjugate(A)
 inverse = (1/det) * adj
 
 RETURN (True, inverse)

FUNCTION ComputeDeterminant(A):
 n = A.rows
 IF n == 1:
 RETURN A[0][0]
 IF n == 2:
 RETURN A[0][0]*A[1][1] - A[0][1]*A[1][0]
 // Use Laplace expansion or LU decomposition for larger n

// Example: A = [[4,7],[2,6]]
(inv_exists, inv_matrix) = IsInvertibleAndInverse([[4,7],[2,6]])
// det = 24-14 = 10 ≠ 0, invertible
// inverse = (1/10)*[[6,-7],[-2,4]] = [[0.6,-0.7],[-0.2,0.4]]
explanation: For a square matrix A representing transformation T, invertibility requires det(A) ≠ 0. The inverse matrix gives T⁻¹, satisfying TT⁻¹ = I.
